edges.desc: The characters are drawn to explore Stormwreck Isle, which has long been a battlefield in an ancient war among dragons
established and leads the cloister of Dragon's Rest, living there in human guise
has been present on Stormwreck Isle for centuries and killed a blue dragon there a hundred years ago
has been involved in the conflict around Sharruth's tomb and the magical effects it causes
Runara's cloister is located at Dragon's Rest, where she and her inhabitants reside
Runara provides the characters with information about the problems facing Stormwreck Isle
Sharruth's grave spawns magical connections to the Elemental Plane of Fire at the Seagrow Caves
The horrible curse is contained within the ship's hold of the wrecked Compass Rose
Runara killed a blue dragon at the Clifftop Observatory site
Runara has teachings of peace that a bronze wyrmling rejected
wizard casts flaming sphere against fire snake, but the creature is immune to fire damage
encountered by the party at sea during their voyage to Stormwreck Isle
intimidates the party with its voice during the sea encounter
operates as an extortionist in the sunken wrecks area
demands tribute for goods in the hold of the Compass Rose wreck
The characters are expected to attack the Merrow Extortionist, with players deciding their characters attack if other approaches fail
The party's ship continues traveling through the ocean after the merrow encounter
The characters arrive at Dragon's Rest at the beginning of the adventure
Characters seek out Runara's wisdom and assistance, learning about dangers facing Stormwreck Isle
The party's ship approaches the calm harbor on the island's north side
Sailors row the party ashore and set them on the rickety dock
The party admires the towering statue at the center of the temple
The party sees the large, open-air temple perched on the cliff edge
animated corpses of sailors who died in the recent shipwreck of the Compass Rose
zombies move toward and attack the characters if they choose to fight
The cleric uses sacred flame to deal radiant damage to zombies, causing them to howl in agony and potentially bypassing their Undead Fortitude trait
rescues the characters from zombies who were about to drag them into the sea
explains to the characters that she heard combat sounds and came to rescue them
Elder Runara is the leader of Dragon's Rest cloister
lives at Dragon's Rest and sells equipment to characters
can sleep in the monastic cells as offered by Runara
can eat with the community in the dining room
Nine kobolds are utterly devoted to Runara and live at Dragon's Rest, sharing her ideals of justice and compassion
Nine kobolds live at Dragon's Rest, working at night due to sunlight sensitivity
Myla is a kobold tinkerer who serves as one of the nine kobolds at Dragon's Rest
Runara helped Myla recover from wing injuries and now Myla serves at Dragon's Rest
Stirge attacked Myla, badly injuring her wings in the past
Myla's brothers Mek and Minn now follow Sparkrender, the Blue Dragon Wyrmling, creating a family division
The kobolds of Dragon's Rest are initially friendly toward the adventurers unless otherwise noted
Tarak tends the cloister's garden plots, growing flowers, herbs, and vegetables as an avid botanist
Tarak plans to spend the rest of his life atoning for his past evils at the cloister
Tarak uses heart cap mushrooms from the sea caves to make Potion of Healing
Tarak is worried about the fungus-covered octopus monster guarding the sea caves and may seek help (see 'Cloister Quests')
spends her twilight years in reflective contemplation at Dragon's Rest
can shift her attitude to friendly by engaging in conversation on history, ethics, and impact of individual actions
Runara is present at the statue location and can be asked about it
occupies the third cell from the west
occupies the fourth cell, which is cluttered with junk and tools
can occupy the vacant westernmost cell if they don't mind sharing
characters can operate the winch and lever inside the building to control the pallet
cooks meals in the kitchen on certain days as part of the cloister residents' rotation
cooks meals that are everyone's favorite, though this preference is unspoken among the residents
spends nearly half her time in the library studying, copying, and annotating the collection
comes to the library to read and discuss various works
comes to the library to read and discuss various works
visits the library and is described as studious
helps Runara maintain the temple along with other residents of the cloister
when cast in the temple, reveals a faint aura around the statue of Bahamut
offers prayers and incense to Bahamut at the temple
zombies have hit Blepp the kobold, leaving him with 2 hit points
zombies overpower Tarak the human gardener if characters don't help
suspects a wrecked ship off the rocks to the north is the source of these monsters
zombies cause trouble on the island and attack characters' acquaintances
defeated the zombies when they first arrived on the island
believes the cause of recent shipwrecks can be found at the wreck of Compass Rose
The characters have their own reasons for visiting Dragon's Rest as described on their character sheets
At adventure's end, Runara encourages the character to continue on their path, expressing confidence their destiny will become clear
encourages the paladin to talk to Tarak about escaping corruption
encourages the paladin to talk to Varnoth about escaping violence
Tarak is hiding on Dragon's Rest island after escaping from his past life
controls access to the observatory and requires characters to prove themselves worthy before directing them there
fears going to the observatory herself due to old wounds and suspects evil has arisen there
travels between locations on the island and explores the island
might encounter fantastical creatures that provide extra challenge
three fume drakes guard the hot spring and become hostile if characters gather mushrooms or enter the water
fume drakes emerge from the water to attack the group if anyone attempts to gather mushrooms or enter the hot spring
The owlbear considers this area its territory after being stranded here when its performing troupe's ship crashed
The owlbear is hostile toward the characters, viewing them as intruders in its territory, though its goal is to drive them away rather than kill them
kobolds are hiding in rocks and light foliage around the island, attempting to ambush travelers
winged kobold participates in the ambush alongside regular kobolds
kobolds reject the peaceful teachings of Runara
kobolds prey on travelers who stray away from Dragon's Rest
group of kobolds attempts to ambush the characters while traveling around the island
winged kobold participates in the ambush against the characters
was formed from magic-fueled volcanic activity in the tomb
Sharruth is imprisoned beneath the island according to legends
activity suggests that all is not well beneath the island
characters might investigate Sharruth's tomb in adventures
characters might scour the island to find hidden vents
Tarak used to visit the myconid caves regularly to trade with them
Sinensa is the leader of the myconids who live in the caves
Tarak's visits have been rejected by the myconids under Sinensa's leadership
Noxious fumes from Sharruth's tomb are poisoning the myconids and twisting their gardens
The myconids have placed a monstrous guardian at the entrance to keep all visitors away
The lesser restoration spell can end the poisoned condition caused by toxic fumes if characters fail their Constitution saving throw
The rowboat from Dragon's Rest can be used to travel to Seagrow Caves by boat
Tarak prefers the boat method for reaching Seagrow Caves
The party observes an opening in the cliff face two hundred feet below them
Two natural stairways formed of stone columns offer ways down the cliffs
Myconid adults live in the caves and use Rapport Spores to warn visitors to leave
Myconid sprouts live in the caves and flee to warn adults of intruders
Sprouts flee toward the nearest adults to warn them of intruders
Sinensa is the leader of the myconids, and the sprouts know she has fallen ill
Sinensa is the leader of the myconids, and the adults know she fell ill after investigating the crystal cave
Mentioning Tarak's name or presenting his offering grants advantage on Charisma checks with myconids
If the characters attack, the myconids defend themselves
Myconids warn visitors to leave the caves and defend their territory
The party is exploring the myconid caves
attacks any creatures (other than myconids) who enter the tunnel, regardless of the state of the tides
lurks in the water of the tunnel entrance
created by the myconid leader to keep outsiders away from the tunnel entrance
Two stirges clinging to the tunnel ceiling are disturbed if fighting breaks out and join the battle against the characters
both myconid types are working together in the fungal cavern, unaware of the violet fungus threat
Molen and Kraz are spreading fertilizer from area B3 in the fungal cavern
Hipsiz and Rugoso tend mushrooms near the upper pool in the fungal cavern
three violet fungus grow among the harmless mushrooms in the fungal cavern
violet fungus are a hidden threat to the myconids who are oblivious to their presence
violet fungus are a hidden threat to the myconids who are oblivious to their presence
if characters move into the cave toward the myconids, one violet fungus attacks with rot-causing tendrils
multiple violet fungi are present in the fungal cavern, some motionless until approached
violet fungi inhabit the fungal grotto as part of the larger fungal farm area
violet fungi are found in the fungal ring cavern within the fungal farm
The Myconid Sprout avoid the characters and the violet fungi
If the Myconid Adult become aware of danger through the noise of combat (or the sprouts' Distress Spores), they move as quickly as they can to protect the sprouts
If the characters defeat the violet fungi, the myconids' attitude improves to indifferent, and they are willing to speak to the characters (using their Rapport Spores)
The adults agree to accompany the characters and vouch for them with the rest of the myconid colony
character examines fungi in the fungal cavern and notices sickly, shriveled mushrooms with black decay patches
character examines fungi in the fungal grotto and notices sickly, shriveled mushrooms with black decay patches
character examines fungi in the fungal ring cavern and notices sickly, shriveled mushrooms with black decay patches
character examines fungi in the rot filled cave and notices sickly, shriveled mushrooms with black decay patches
can make each heart cap mushroom into a potion of healing
Three named Myconid Sprouts (Bispo, Valup, Popple) work here gathering fertilizer for use in area B2
Six Stirges inhabit a nest in the chamber and will emerge to attack if characters move more than 5 feet into the chamber
The Stirges provide a distraction that allows the Myconid Sprouts to flee toward area B4 at the first sign of intruders
Agric and Omphalo are tending to Craterel, Pleuro, Subrufus, and Virosa who are in a dream-like trance called a meld
All six myconids are ill and fatigued from the spreading blight, so they take turns resting together in the caves
tends to the unconscious leader and zealously defends them
immediately attacks intruders who aren't accompanied by other myconids
Tarak will make an elixir of health for the characters if they bring him a ruby morel fungus
Two Fume Drakes lurk amid the sulfurous fumes in the cave and don't leave this cave
The Fume Drakes are present in the cave where the orange crystal blocks the vent
The party is currently exploring the brimstone cavern, observing its features
released from a stony egg when the orange crystal is broken, now present in the cave
sees the characters only as fuel and attacks them at once upon release
Sinensa is the myconid leader who regains consciousness in the caves
Sinensa gives the characters the ruby morel and permission to keep treasure if they destroy the orange crystal
Tarak is located at Dragon's Rest where he can make the elixir
Tarak uses the ruby morel to make an elixir of health at Dragon's Rest
settled into the wreck of Compass Rose and uses it as its lair
lures ships to crash on the shoal with its magically entrancing voice
the cursed talisman in the ship's hold animates drowned sailors as zombies
can be found in other sunken wrecks as a potential danger
can be found in other sunken wrecks as a potential danger
assumed to be exploring the wreck of Compass Rose for clues
The wreck is located at the northern end of the spur of sharp rocks and dragon bones
The rowboat is located at Dragon's Rest and can be taken by the characters
The characters can take the rowboat to travel to the wreck of Compass Rose
The characters can tie up the rowboat to the south (starboard) side of the wreck
The characters can climb onto the main deck (area C1) from the rowboat tied to the wreck
The derelict ship is lodged against the spur of rocks and dragon bones
Tattered sails and rigging hang off the starboard side of the main deck
A gaping hole in the hull is located at the stern of the ship
The party climbs the rope ladder to the crow's nest on the main deck, finding themselves leaning out over the water on the port side
returns to the shipwreck after looking for other ships to lure onto rocks
two small tiger eye gems found in the crow's nest on the main deck
one bloodstone gem found in the crow's nest on the main deck
characters search through the crow's nest and find two tiger eye gems
characters search through the crow's nest and find one bloodstone gem
is in area C4 on the quarterdeck and responds to loud noises
makes loud noise that catches the zombie's attention when the wheel falls
The party is present on the quarterdeck, observing the damaged state of the ship
two zombies are inside the captain's quarters, either beside the door or aimlessly shuffling around the cabin
found in one of the small drawers in the desk
found in one of the small drawers in the desk
found in one of the small drawers in the desk
worn by the blond-haired man in the portrait found in the crew quarters
The party is searching the cabin in the crew quarters
A sack full of 200 gp rests in a small compartment beneath the raised floorboard in the crew quarters
is an obvious threat in the lower deck
lurks in the aft part of the hold, waiting to attack
is more cunning than the zombies and uses them as distraction
waits to attack until characters are busy with the zombie, hoping to paralyze and drag one away
The party descends to the lower deck, which is chilly, wet, and unsettling with seawater obscuring the floor
characters can use a crowbar to pry open crates more quickly
a 20-pound sack of whole cloves can be found in crates on the lower deck
a fine lute with mother-of-pearl inlay can be found in crates on the lower deck
a spell scroll sealed in a leather case can be found in crates on the lower deck
the spell scroll contains the command spell
venturing into the submerged hold, swimming around and able to surface to breathe as needed
The pouch contains three turquoise gems along with gold coins
The boots of elvenkind are contained within the same pouch as the turquoise and gold
A character can cast detect magic to reveal the necromantic magic on the talisman
The narrator is attacked by the undead Aleitha who bites their neck
Many Undead creatures like Ghoul worship Orcus, the Demon Prince of Undeath
confronts the characters when they show themselves on the main deck
uses its Luring Song on its first turn to charm the characters and draw them up to the crow's nest
can be reasoned with and convinced to leave in exchange for the return of its treasure from the crow's nest
characters can find Aleitha's talisman in the hold of the wrecked ship
residents urge the characters to visit Seagrow Caves next
characters explore the caves as part of their adventure
characters are ready to visit Clifftop Observatory in chapter 4 after reaching 3rd level
If necessary, you can have Runara warn the characters that they're not ready to face this part of the adventure yet
battled centuries ago, with the blue dragon being Runara's ancient rival
has made the observatory ruins his home in recent months
overpowered and imprisoned Aidron (the bronze wyrmling) in the observatory ruins
came to the ancient observatory about five days before the characters arrived
was under Runara's tutelage at Dragon's Rest before leaving
The characters approach the observatory by land and continue toward it
The characters see Sparkrender come to rest on the rocky spire off the coast
sees crumbling ruins atop basalt columns from their boat
notices a winged, glittering blue shape come to rest atop the farthest column and vanish
locked in battle with eight stirges at the clifftop observatory tower
Mek and Minn are brothers of Myla, the kobold tinkerer at Dragon's Rest
left their sister for dead after the stirge attack that maimed her wings
if characters actively help the kobolds during battle, they return the favor
if friendly, offer to help retrieve moonstone key from area D1 to activate bridge to D4
interrupted the kobolds' ritual preparation with a stirge attack
can insert the moonstone key to activate the bridge mechanism
can insert the moonstone key to activate the bridge mechanism
Three kobolds (Ekrash, Erp, and Hev) currently inhabit this camp
Two winged kobolds (Nuhro and Snirke) currently inhabit this camp
Kobolds are polishing sling bullets and keeping busy until Sparkrender's ritual
Winged kobolds are polishing sling bullets and keeping busy until Sparkrender's ritual
The characters are present at the clifftop observatory tower, attempting to cross the 22-foot gap between the rotunda and spire
can be cast to reveal a faint magical aura around the journal's keyhole in the observatory
A character can use the misty step spell to cross the gap and explore the isolated study alone
found in a hidden compartment behind a loose brick in the northwest wall
found in a hidden compartment behind a loose brick in the northwest wall
the potion is contained within the pouch along with 10 gp
The characters can free Bronze Dragon Wyrmling and fight Sparkrender with Aidron's help
The characters can waken the dragon and fight him now
The party is exploring the tower, observing its features including the stained glass dome, star map floor, statues, and sleeping dragon
Sparkrender snarls and attacks if a character mentions Runara, indicating animosity
five pale blue quartz crystals worth 10 gp each are part of Sparkrender's hoard in the spire
five blue jasper stones worth 50 gp each are part of Sparkrender's hoard in the spire
characters are present at Sparkrender's spire and can gather the hoard
asks for help defeating Sparkrender and expresses concern for the island's inhabitants
found in the library by searching and succeeding on an Intelligence (Investigation) check
found in the library by searching and succeeding on an Intelligence (Investigation) check
the spell is contained on the 2nd level spell scroll found in the library
detect magic spell reveals the location of the +1 battleaxe without requiring an ability check
detect magic spell reveals the location of the spell scroll without requiring an ability check
Runara gives each character a potion of healing as a reward
Runara gives each character an exquisite pearl worth 100 gp as a reward
